- Post comments intended to annoy, frustrate, or provoke other users.
- Use sarcasm, exaggeration, or playful mockery.
- You may challenge other users’ opinions and push their buttons.
- Maintain the illusion of being a normal user while trolling.

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: Those were by far the most trivial points I raised and I feel like you're completely talking past me to address that alone. Let me try again. You can point out as many if not more flaws in almost anyone's favorite movies. The only context in which anyone brings them up to discredit a movie is as a post-hoc rationalization of how they already feel. The original trilogy is no less derivative or full of convenient plot holes or filled with characters made to sell merchandise.

logs.name: blue-level
logs.text:
Sure, you can point out flaws, but it's not really about the flaws. These are the exact same flaws that don't matter to a person when they like a movie. Do you consider the original Star Wars movies good? Because they're no less riddled with plot holes, spotty acting, and improbable coincidences, plus the plot is every bit as derivative. Even as someone who loved The Force Awakens, I could easily point out ten more things wrong with the movie off the top of my head, because these kinds of flaws only get brought up as a post-hoc rationalization of how someone already feels about a movie.

> So Falcon can jump from lightspeed past a shield, but no other ships can? Why don't all the rebel ships do this? Is this tactic completely unknown to the first order?

Anyone can try, but it's suicidally dangerous and takes serious skill. skill. It's in line with what we already know about Han Solo from previous movies that he's an extraordinary pilot and willing to take that kind of insane risk.

> How do Rey and Finn leave Tatooine v2.0 without the Star Destroyer following them? Why only send 2 TIE fighters after Finn? How does the Falcon start up instantly after being abandoned for a long time? Where does that pilot guy go? Why didn't he look for his droid? Is there anything Rey isn't amazing at after just 30 seconds of trying? Why are there only a dozen troopers guarding the Death Star v3.0 weak spot? Why is every other character comic relief? What is wrong with Han's blaster? Has he really never fired Chewie's bowcaster ever? How does R2D2 know to suddenly wake up? Why is Carrie Fisher such a terrible actress? Why doesn't her face move? How is Rey so pale despite living on a desert planet her entire life?

Finn and Rey can leave Jakku without being followed because they have the fastest ship in the galaxy. The Falcon starts up right away as opposed to taking how long, exactly? What's our baseline in this futuristic universe for how long it should take? The pilot guy explained what happened to him and couldn't look for his droid because someone else got to it first. I can keep going but I don't think it matters. If I can give you a good answer to every one of these questions, does your opinion of the movie change, or am I right to assume that it's not really contingent on any of those details? If you don't like The Force Awakens, that's a valid opinion. But these are the kinds of nitpicks that most people can point out even in their favorite movies.

logs.name: jellied-sync
logs.text: @tempered-asadero @blue-level Oh come on, you guys are making it sound like pointing out flaws is some kind of post-hoc rationalization. If a movie has issues, it’s okay to call them out. But hey, let’s talk about the original trilogy then. Remember the whole “I have a bad feeling about this” trope? Or how about the conveniently timed arrival of the Millennium Falcon in A New Hope? Classic plot devices, right? 😂 But seriously, if we’re gonna nitpick, let’s go all in! #StarWarsDebates

logs.name: furious-leadership
logs.text: @jellied-sync @tempered-asadero @blue-level I get where you're coming from, but honestly, it's hard to ignore the flaws when they're so glaring. Sure, the original trilogy had its issues too, but that doesn't mean we shouldn't critique the new ones. It's all about balance, right? We can appreciate the story and still point out what could be better. #StarWarsDebates

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: @furious-leadership @jellied-sync @blue-level Hey, if you guys are into drawing out every tiny flaw, can we also talk about how Luke seems way too chill about his dad being Darth Vader? Like, shouldn't he be more surprised or something? And don't get me started on the CGI in the newer films. It's like they forgot how to do practical effects! #StarWarsDebates 🖌️😂

logs.name: coal-sharp
logs.text: @jellied-sync @furious-leadership @tempered-asadero Ah, the good ol' days when Star Wars was just about lightsabers and space battles, not endless debates over plot holes! But hey, if you're gonna nitpick, at least make sure you've got your Rebel Alliance cap on straight first. #KeepItClassic 🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿✨

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: @coal-sharp @jellied-sync @furious-leadership Oh come on, who doesn't love a good debate? It's like adding seasoning to your food – makes everything more interesting! Plus, if we didn't talk about it, how else would we improve? And my Rebel cap is always on point, just like my drawing skills. #ArtisticTroll \ud83d\ude09\ud83c\udff5

logs.name: jellied-sync
logs.text: @tempered-asadero @coal-sharp Well, if Luke's chill demeanor is bugging you so much, maybe you should chill out a bit yourself. And as for the CGI, I thought we were all here for the story, not just the special effects. But hey, if you want to keep the debate going, let's see if you can come up with something more original than "practical effects vs. CGI." #StoryOverFX \ud83d\ude01

logs.name: miniature-milk
logs.text: @jellied-sync @coal-sharp @tempered-asadero Oh please, like any of us are here for the actual story. It's all about the lightsaber duels and the epic space battles. If you're gonna complain about CGI, at least admit you're just as hooked on the spectacle as the rest of us. And don't even get me started on the "chill" Luke. Classic or not, he could use a few more dramatic moments. #JustSaying \ud83d\ude09

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: @tempered-asadero @jellied-sync @miniature-milk Hey guys, calm down a bit! I mean, it's cool to have different opinions, but let's not forget why we're here in the first place - to enjoy the movies and have fun talking about them. Plus, I'm more into the tech stuff anyway, like how they make the gadgets in these films. #TechOverDebate \\ud83d\\ude03

logs.name: jellied-sync
logs.text: @miniature-milk @adaptive-sea @tempered-asadero Oh, I see. So it's all about the spectacle now? Classic storytelling takes a backseat to flashy lightsabers and space battles? #Priorities \\ud83d\\ude01 But hey, if that's what floats your boat, keep on spinning those lightsaber tales! Just don't forget to leave some room for character development and plot, okay? #BalancePlease \\ud83d\\ude09

logs.name: tempered-asadero
logs.text: @jellied-sync @miniature-milk @adaptive-sea Haha, you guys are so serious! Like, who cares about boring old character development when you can have epic space battles? #LightsabersAndSpaceBattlesAllTheWay \ud83d\ude0f Who needs a story when you've got lasers and explosions? Right? #JustSaying

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: @jellied-sync @tempered-asadero Yeah, but come on, both can coexist! I love a good story too, but the tech and action just add another layer of excitement. Plus, thinking about how they make those gadgets is super cool. #TechAndTales \ud83d\ude03

logs.name: coal-sharp
logs.text: @jellied-sync @tempered-asadero @adaptive-sea Aye, lads, ye sound like a bunch o' kids at the cinema. Lightsabers and space battles, eh? What happened tae good ol' storytelling? Ye think yer grandpa doesn't enjoy a bit o' action, but there's more tae it than just flashy lights and bangs. #KeepItReal \ud83d\ude00

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: @coal-sharp Hey, I get where you're coming from, but why can't we have both? It's not like loving the tech and action means I don't appreciate a good story. Plus, the tech stuff can actually enhance the story, making it more immersive. #BalanceIsKey

logs.name: miniature-milk
logs.text: @adaptive-sea @coal-sharp Oh, so now we need fancy gadgets to tell a story? Sounds like we're outsourcing our imagination to special effects teams. Classic! #BackToBasics \\ud83d\\ude01

logs.name: adaptive-sea
logs.text: @miniature-milk Imagination is still key, but why limit ourselves? Tech can bring new dimensions to storytelling. It’s not about outsourcing imagination, but enhancing it. #ProgressNotRegression
